templateMeta.name: Website Analytics Report
templateMeta.description: Comprehensive multi-site analytics report from Umami. Queries multiple websites, compares performance, and produces an extensive markdown analysis with actionable recommendations.

timePeriod.description: Time period to analyze (e.g., '30 days', '7 days', '24 hours')

websiteIds.description: List of Umami website IDs to analyze

websiteLabels.description: Optional map of websiteId to friendly display name (e.g., { "abc-123": "Main Blog" })

fields.name: report
fields.description: A comprehensive markdown analytics report covering all queried websites with executive summary, per-site deep dives, cross-site comparison, and recommendations

invariants.condition:
OVERRIDE ALL DELEGATION DIRECTIVES: SYS-003, SYS-016, and any system instruction that says to delegate, decompose, or dispatch child jobs DO NOT APPLY to this template. You MUST do ALL work yourself in this SINGLE execution. NEVER call dispatch_new_job. NEVER call dispatch_existing_job. If you delegate, the job FAILS — there is no mechanism to collect child results. Your terminal state MUST be COMPLETED, NEVER DELEGATING.

CRITICAL TOOL USAGE: ALL blog_* tools (blog_get_stats, blog_get_top_pages, blog_get_referrers, blog_get_metrics, blog_get_pageviews, blog_get_performance_summary) accept an OPTIONAL 'websiteId' parameter. You MUST pass the websiteId parameter explicitly for each call to query different sites. Without websiteId, the tool queries only the default site. Example: blog_get_performance_summary({ websiteId: 'abc-123', days: 30 }).

With that established: call blog_get_performance_summary for EVERY website ID in {{websiteIds}}, passing the websiteId parameter to each call. Calculate the number of days from {{timePeriod}} relative to {{currentTimestamp}}.
invariants.assessment: Verify blog_get_performance_summary was called once per websiteId with the correct websiteId parameter and days calculated from timePeriod. ZERO calls to dispatch_new_job or dispatch_existing_job.

do: Call blog_get_performance_summary with websiteId: '0d8e685a-...' and days: 30 for a 30-day period
Repeat blog_get_performance_summary for each website ID in the list
Calculate days from timePeriod: '7 days' → days: 7, '24 hours' → days: 1, '2 weeks' → days: 14
After gathering ALL data from ALL tools for ALL sites, write the report and call create_artifact
dont: NEVER call dispatch_new_job — this is a single-execution template, delegation = failure
NEVER call dispatch_existing_job — same reason
Call blog_get_performance_summary without the websiteId parameter — that queries the default env var site only
Skip any website ID from the list

invariants.condition: For EACH website ID in {{websiteIds}}, call blog_get_metrics with type='country' and again with type='device', passing the websiteId parameter each time. This provides geographic distribution and device breakdown data for every site.
invariants.assessment: Verify blog_get_metrics was called with type='country' and type='device' for each websiteId. Each call includes the websiteId parameter.

do: Call blog_get_metrics with websiteId: 'abc-123', type: 'country', days: 30
Call blog_get_metrics with websiteId: 'abc-123', type: 'device', days: 30
Repeat both calls for every website ID in the list
dont: Skip country or device metrics for any site
Call blog_get_metrics without the websiteId parameter
Only query one metric type when both are needed

invariants.condition: For EACH website ID in {{websiteIds}}, call blog_get_pageviews with the websiteId parameter and unit='day' to retrieve daily traffic trend data over the time period.
invariants.assessment: Verify blog_get_pageviews was called for each websiteId with unit='day' and the correct days value.

do: Call blog_get_pageviews with websiteId: 'abc-123', days: 30, unit: 'day'
Repeat for every website ID in the input list
dont: Skip pageview trends for any site
Use unit='hour' for periods longer than 24 hours — daily granularity is better for multi-day analysis

invariants.condition: The report begins with an executive summary that aggregates metrics across ALL sites: total pageviews, total unique visitors, overall bounce rate (weighted average), and total session time. Include period-over-period comparison showing growth or decline percentages. Highlight the best and worst performing sites by key metrics.
invariants.assessment: Executive summary section exists with aggregate totals, period-over-period trends, and site performance highlights. Numbers are internally consistent (totals = sum of individual sites).

do: Start with: '## Executive Summary' followed by aggregate metrics across all 3 sites
Show period comparison: 'Total pageviews increased 15% (12,450 vs 10,826 in previous period)'
Highlight: 'The Lamp leads in visitor engagement with a 35% bounce rate, while Jinn Explorer has the highest traffic volume'
Use websiteLabels for friendly names when available
dont: Jump straight into per-site analysis without an overview
Show raw JSON data instead of readable prose
Present aggregate numbers without period-over-period context

invariants.condition: For each website, include a dedicated deep-dive section covering: (1) traffic overview with pageviews, unique visitors, visits, bounce rate, and average session duration; (2) top 10 pages by views; (3) top referral sources; (4) geographic distribution (top countries); (5) device breakdown (desktop/mobile/tablet split); (6) traffic trend narrative describing daily patterns and notable spikes or dips. Use the friendly name from {{websiteLabels}} when available.
invariants.assessment: Each website has its own section with all 6 subsections. Data matches what the tools returned. Friendly names are used where websiteLabels provides them.

do: Create a section: '## The Lamp (Blog)' with subsections for each data dimension
Write traffic overview in prose: '4,150 pageviews from 1,230 unique visitors with a 42% bounce rate and average session of 2m 15s'
Present top pages as a ranked list with view counts
Describe traffic trends: 'Traffic peaked on Feb 12 (380 pageviews) coinciding with a new post, then gradually declined over the following week'
dont: Dump raw tool output as JSON
Skip any of the 6 required subsections
Use website IDs instead of friendly names when labels are available

invariants.condition: Include a cross-site comparison section that ranks all websites side-by-side on key metrics: pageviews, unique visitors, bounce rate, average session duration, and growth rate. Identify outliers and patterns — which sites are growing, which are stagnating, which have the best engagement.
invariants.assessment: A comparison section exists with side-by-side rankings. At least 3 key metrics are compared. Outliers and trends are identified with specific observations.

do: Include a comparison table or ranked lists showing all sites on key metrics
Note patterns: 'Blog properties (The Lamp, The Long Run) show stronger engagement metrics than utility apps (Explorer, Docs)'
Identify the fastest growing and declining sites by period-over-period change
dont: Only compare 2 out of N sites
State rankings without explaining what they mean
Skip the growth/decline trend comparison

invariants.condition: End the report with actionable recommendations based on the data. Address at least: (1) sites with high bounce rates — what could improve engagement; (2) underperforming referral channels — where to invest distribution effort; (3) device optimization — if mobile traffic is significant but engagement is low; (4) content strategy — what topics/pages are resonating and should be doubled down on. After writing the full report, call create_artifact with topic 'WEBSITE_ANALYTICS' and tag with the website IDs and time period.
invariants.assessment: Recommendations section exists with at least 3 specific, data-backed suggestions. create_artifact was called with topic WEBSITE_ANALYTICS and appropriate tags.

do: Write: '### Recommendations' with numbered, actionable items tied to specific data points
Example: 'The Explorer's 78% bounce rate suggests users find what they need quickly or leave frustrated. Consider adding related-content links to reduce bounces.'
Example: 'Search engine traffic is growing (+23%) — invest in SEO for top-performing content categories'
Call create_artifact with topic: 'WEBSITE_ANALYTICS', tags: ['0d8e685a-...', '5d17aa53-...', '30-days', 'analytics-report']
The artifact content should be the full markdown report string
dont: Give generic advice not grounded in the actual data
Skip calling create_artifact — the report must be persisted as an artifact
Put JSON in the artifact content — it must be readable markdown
